commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
A leper. In punishment of his usurping the priestly functions, 2 Paralipomenon xxvi. 19. (Challoner) (Worthington)
The priests boldly remonstrated with him, but to no effect; till the king perceived himself stricken with the leprosy. Josephus ( Amos i. 1., and Zacharias xiv. 5; (St. Jerome) though Usher produces some chronological difficulties against this opinion: but they are founded on error. (Calmet)
Free. Paralipomenon apart; at a distance from all. (Haydock)
The like rules were observed in cities, as in the camp, Leviticus xiii. 46. (Menochius)
Ozias was in a manner dead to all civil transactions; and Isaias (vi. 1.) probably refers to this event. Syriac and Arabic, "he remained hidden. "Josephus (ibid.) says, "leading a private life. "Thus aiming at too much, he lost all! (Haydock)

commentaries.author: Richard Challoner
commentaries.content: A leper: In punishment of his usurping the priestly function. 2 Par. 26.
